title: Robot 'Decapitated' in World's First-Ever Humanoid UFC Fight - Slashdot
excerpt: "A humanoid robot lost its head," reports Newsweek, "during the world's first free-combat tournament for full-sized humanoid robots." The Ultimate Robot Knock-out Legend competition began Thursday in Shenzhen, China, according to the article, with local robotics company EngineAI providing $40,000 ...
content_html:
"A humanoid robot lost its head," reports Newsweek, "during the world's first free-combat tournament for full-sized humanoid robots."

The Ultimate Robot Knock-out Legend competition began Thursday in Shenzhen, China, according to the article, with local robotics company EngineAI providing $40,000 of their "T800" robots (yes, named after The Terminator) to 32 participating teams from around the world: A video shared of the combat on YouTube by local news outlet Shenzhen Story, showed that even after one of the robots had its head practically knocked off its shoulders, it continued to fight, throwing punches at its opponent and kicking into the air... [White humanoid robot "White Eagle"] landed a high kick to the head of its black opponent, "Matador," which made the robot's head rock precariously in its socket before rolling completely out of place. The two continued to spar as Matador's head was swinging from its socket until eventually the robot fell, crushing its head underneath its body.

Matador tried to scramble back to its feet, but its head flew off and the robot then collapsed back down. The White Eagle did a celebratory dance for the crowd as the fight concluded, and did a move that mimicked that of someone flexing their biceps. The White Eagle waited in the ring, fists still up, as Matador was carried away, occasionally doing a few more dance moves...

Per a report by Global Times, the winning team will be awarded a gold championship belt worth $1.44 million (10 million yuan) by the event organizer.


It's a strange fight. The robots sometimes seem unaware of where their opponent is, facing the wrong direction or throwing kicks and punches in the air. In the first round White Eagle just knocks over Matador, who then isn't able to stand back up. (And White Eagle again appears to do a victorious dance.)

EngineAI's site says they aim to "promote the development of robot combat events toward greater professionalism, scale, and industrialization," while fostering innovation and global collaboration.

Thanks to Slashdot reader pbahra for sharing the news.
content_text: "A humanoid robot lost its head," reports Newsweek, "during the world's first free-combat tournament for full-sized humanoid robots." The Ultimate Robot Knock-out Legend competition began Thursday in Shenzhen, China, according to the article, with local robotics company EngineAI providing $40,000 of their "T800" robots (yes, named after The Terminator) to 32 participating teams from around the world: A video shared of the combat on YouTube by local news outlet Shenzhen Story, showed that even after one of the robots had its head practically knocked off its shoulders, it continued to fight, throwing punches at its opponent and kicking into the air... [White humanoid robot "White Eagle"] landed a high kick to the head of its black opponent, "Matador," which made the robot's head rock precariously in its socket before rolling completely out of place. The two continued to spar as Matador's head was swinging from its socket until eventually the robot fell, crushing its head underneath its body. Matador tried to scramble back to its feet, but its head flew off and the robot then collapsed back down. The White Eagle did a celebratory dance for the crowd as the fight concluded, and did a move that mimicked that of someone flexing their biceps. The White Eagle waited in the ring, fists still up, as Matador was carried away, occasionally doing a few more dance moves... Per a report by Global Times, the winning team will be awarded a gold championship belt worth $1.44 million (10 million yuan) by the event organizer. It's a strange fight. The robots sometimes seem unaware of where their opponent is, facing the wrong direction or throwing kicks and punches in the air. In the first round White Eagle just knocks over Matador, who then isn't able to stand back up. (And White Eagle again appears to do a victorious dance.) EngineAI's site says they aim to "promote the development of robot combat events toward greater professionalism, scale, and industrialization," while fostering innovation and global collaboration. sentences.en: "A humanoid robot lost its head," reports Newsweek, "during the world's first free-combat tournament for full-sized humanoid robots."
sentences.zh: 《新闻周刊》报道称，“在世界首届全尺寸人形机器人自由搏击锦标赛中，一个人形机器人失去了头部。”

blocks.html_en:
The Ultimate Robot Knock-out Legend competition began Thursday in Shenzhen, China, according to the article, with local robotics company EngineAI providing $40,000 of their "T800" robots (yes, named after The Terminator) to 32 participating teams from around the world: A video shared of the combat on YouTube by local news outlet Shenzhen Story, showed that even after one of the robots had its head practically knocked off its shoulders, it continued to fight, throwing punches at its opponent and kicking into the air... [White humanoid robot "White Eagle"] landed a high kick to the head of its black opponent, "Matador," which made the robot's head rock precariously in its socket before rolling completely out of place. The two continued to spar as Matador's head was swinging from its socket until eventually the robot fell, crushing its head underneath its body.

Matador tried to scramble back to its feet, but its head flew off and the robot then collapsed back down. The White Eagle did a celebratory dance for the crowd as the fight concluded, and did a move that mimicked that of someone flexing their biceps. The White Eagle waited in the ring, fists still up, as Matador was carried away, occasionally doing a few more dance moves...

Per a report by Global Times, the winning team will be awarded a gold championship belt worth $1.44 million (10 million yuan) by the event organizer.


It's a strange fight. The robots sometimes seem unaware of where their opponent is, facing the wrong direction or throwing kicks and punches in the air. In the first round White Eagle just knocks over Matador, who then isn't able to stand back up. (And White Eagle again appears to do a victorious dance.)

sentences.en: The Ultimate Robot Knock-out Legend competition began Thursday in Shenzhen, China, according to the article, with local robotics company EngineAI providing $40,000 of their "T800" robots (yes, named after The Terminator) to 32 participating teams from around the world: A video shared of the combat on YouTube by local news outlet Shenzhen Story, showed that even after one of the robots had its head practically knocked off its shoulders, it continued to fight, throwing punches at its opponent and kicking into the air... [White humanoid robot "White Eagle"] landed a high kick to the head of its black opponent, "Matador," which made the robot's head rock precariously in its socket before rolling completely out of place.
sentences.zh: 文章称，终极机器人淘汰赛传奇比赛周四在中国深圳拉开帷幕，当地机器人公司 EngineAI 向来自世界各地的 32 支参赛队伍提供了价值 4 万美元的“T800”机器人（是的，以终结者的名字命名）：当地新闻媒体《深圳故事》在 YouTube 上分享了一段战斗视频，显示即使其中一个机器人的头几乎从肩膀上被撞下来，它仍然继续战斗，向对手拳打脚踢。腾空而起……【白色人形机器人“白鹰”】对着黑人对手“斗牛士”的头部狠狠踢了一脚，机器人的头部在插座中摇摇欲坠，然后完全滚出原位。

sentences.en: The two continued to spar as Matador's head was swinging from its socket until eventually the robot fell, crushing its head underneath its body.
sentences.zh: 两人继续打斗，斗牛士的头从插座中摆动，直到机器人最终摔倒，头部压在身体下面。

sentences.en: Matador tried to scramble back to its feet, but its head flew off and the robot then collapsed back down.
sentences.zh: 斗牛士试图爬起来，但它的头飞走了，机器人随后又倒了下来。

sentences.en: The White Eagle did a celebratory dance for the crowd as the fight concluded, and did a move that mimicked that of someone flexing their biceps.
sentences.zh: 比赛结束时，白鹰为观众跳了一支庆祝舞蹈，并做了一个模仿某人弯曲二头肌的动作。

sentences.en: The White Eagle waited in the ring, fists still up, as Matador was carried away, occasionally doing a few more dance moves...
sentences.zh: 白鹰在擂台上等待着，拳头依然举起，斗牛士被带走，偶尔还会跳几个舞步……

sentences.en: Per a report by Global Times, the winning team will be awarded a gold championship belt worth $1.44 million (10 million yuan) by the event organizer.
sentences.zh: 据《环球时报》报道，获胜队伍将获得赛事主办方颁发的价值144万美元（1000万元人民币）的金冠军腰带。

sentences.en: The robots sometimes seem unaware of where their opponent is, facing the wrong direction or throwing kicks and punches in the air.
sentences.zh: 机器人有时似乎不知道对手在哪里，面朝错误的方向，或者在空中踢拳。

sentences.en: In the first round White Eagle just knocks over Matador, who then isn't able to stand back up.
sentences.zh: 第一回合，白鹰就击倒了斗牛士，后者再也无法站起来。

sentences.en: (And White Eagle again appears to do a victorious dance.)
sentences.zh: （白鹰再次出现，跳起了胜利的舞蹈。）

blocks.html_en: Matador tried to scramble back to its feet, but its head flew off and the robot then collapsed back down. The White Eagle did a celebratory dance for the crowd as the fight concluded, and did a move that mimicked that of someone flexing their biceps. The White Eagle waited in the ring, fists still up, as Matador was carried away, occasionally doing a few more dance moves...

sentences.zh: 斗牛士试图爬起来，但它的头飞走了，机器人随后又倒了下来。

sentences.zh: 白鹰在擂台上等待着，拳头依然举起，斗牛士被带走，偶尔还会跳几个舞步……

blocks.html_en: Per a report by Global Times, the winning team will be awarded a gold championship belt worth $1.44 million (10 million yuan) by the event organizer.

sentences.en: Per a report by Global Times, the winning team will be awarded a gold championship belt worth $1.44 million (10 million yuan) by the event organizer.
sentences.zh: 据《环球时报》报道，获胜队伍将获得赛事主办方颁发的价值144万美元（1000万元人民币）的金冠军腰带。

blocks.html_en: EngineAI's site says they aim to "promote the development of robot combat events toward greater professionalism, scale, and industrialization," while fostering innovation and global collaboration.

sentences.en: EngineAI's site says they aim to "promote the development of robot combat events toward greater professionalism, scale, and industrialization," while fostering innovation and global collaboration.
sentences.zh: EngineAI 网站称，他们的目标是“促进机器人格斗赛事朝着更高的专业性、规模化和工业化方向发展”，同时促进创新和全球合作。

blocks.html_en: Thanks to Slashdot reader pbahra for sharing the news.

sentences.en: Thanks to Slashdot reader pbahra for sharing the news.
sentences.zh: 感谢 Slashdot 读者 pbahra 分享这个消息。

content_html_zh:
《新闻周刊》报道称，“在世界首届全尺寸人形机器人自由搏击锦标赛中，一个人形机器人失去了头部。”

文章称，终极机器人淘汰赛传奇比赛周四在中国深圳拉开帷幕，当地机器人公司 EngineAI 向来自世界各地的 32 支参赛队伍提供了价值 4 万美元的“T800”机器人（是的，以终结者的名字命名）：当地新闻媒体《深圳故事》在 YouTube 上分享了一段战斗视频，显示即使其中一个机器人的头几乎从肩膀上被撞下来，它仍然继续战斗，向对手拳打脚踢。腾空而起……【白色人形机器人“白鹰”】对着黑人对手“斗牛士”的头部狠狠踢了一脚，机器人的头部在插座中摇摇欲坠，然后完全滚出原位。 两人继续打斗，斗牛士的头从插座中摆动，直到机器人最终摔倒，头部压在身体下面。 斗牛士试图爬起来，但它的头飞走了，机器人随后又倒了下来。 比赛结束时，白鹰为观众跳了一支庆祝舞蹈，并做了一个模仿某人弯曲二头肌的动作。 白鹰在擂台上等待着，拳头依然举起，斗牛士被带走，偶尔还会跳几个舞步…… 据《环球时报》报道，获胜队伍将获得赛事主办方颁发的价值144万美元（1000万元人民币）的金冠军腰带。 这是一场奇怪的战斗。 机器人有时似乎不知道对手在哪里，面朝错误的方向，或者在空中踢拳。 第一回合，白鹰就击倒了斗牛士，后者再也无法站起来。 （白鹰再次出现，跳起了胜利的舞蹈。）

EngineAI 网站称，他们的目标是“促进机器人格斗赛事朝着更高的专业性、规模化和工业化方向发展”，同时促进创新和全球合作。

感谢 Slashdot 读者 pbahra 分享这个消息。
